title: Handle Taxes & Final Returns in California
description: File final individual tax return and estate tax obligations
overview: A final individual tax return must be filed for the year of death. Depending on estate size, additional estate or inheritance tax returns may be required.
state_note: California probate is filed in Superior Court. Estates under $184,500 may qualify for simplified small estate procedures. Community property rules apply to married couples.
steps: Gather tax records and prior year returns
File final individual income tax return (Form 1040)
Note "DECEASED" and date of death on return
Apply for estate EIN if estate has income
File estate income tax return (Form 1041) if required
Determine if estate tax return (Form 706) is required
Check state estate or inheritance tax requirements
Consider working with a CPA or tax attorney

title: File for Probate (if required) in California
description: Begin the probate process with the local court
overview: Probate is the court process that gives someone legal authority to manage the estate. It applies whether or not there is a will. Intestate estates (no will) also go through probate. Requirements vary by state, but the general workflow is consistent.
state_note: California probate is filed in Superior Court. Estates under $184,500 may qualify for simplified small estate procedures. Community property rules apply to married couples.
steps: Gather core documents (death certificate
proof of relationship)
Identify the county where probate is filed (typically where the person lived at time of death)
Determine if the estate qualifies for simplified/small estate procedures (thresholds vary by state
File the initial petition with the probate court
Receive Letters Testamentary (with will) or Letters of Administration (without will)
Notify creditors as required by your state (often by publication and direct mail)
Inventory estate assets and file with the court if required
Track all court deadlines and required notices carefully

overview: An Employer Identification Number (EIN) is like a Social Security number for the estate. You need it to open an estate bank account, file estate tax returns, and conduct estate business. The application is free and takes about 10 minutes online.
state_note: California probate is filed in Superior Court. Estates under $184,500 may qualify for simplified small estate procedures. Community property rules apply to married couples.
steps: Go to IRS.gov and find the online EIN application
Select "Estate" as the entity type
Enter the deceased\'s information and your information as executor
Provide the date of death
Complete the application and receive the EIN immediately
Save the EIN confirmation letter for your records

title: Transfer Vehicle Registration and Title in California
description: Transfer vehicle ownership to a new owner through the DMV
overview: Vehicle titles must be transferred after a death. The process varies by state and depends on how the vehicle was titled (sole owner, joint, or transfer-on-death). Contact your state DMV for specific requirements.
state_note: California probate is filed in Superior Court. Estates under $184,500 may qualify for simplified small estate procedures. Community property rules apply to married couples.
steps: Locate the vehicle title and registration documents
Check how the vehicle is titled — look at the exact names and wording on the title (sole
or trust)
Determine if there is an outstanding loan — check the title for lien notation or contact the lender
Gather required documents: certified death certificate
and Letters Testamentary if going through probate
Visit the DMV to complete the title transfer (or send a delegate with a limited Power of Attorney)
Update the vehicle registration in the new owner\'s name
Contact the auto insurer immediately — the deceased\'s policy does NOT cover new drivers for personal use
Save the new title

overview: Three categories of registered property that executors commonly overlook: vehicles (state DMV), vessels (state for small boats, USCG for documented >5 net tons), and firearms (ATF for NFA items; some states have their own registries). LightPath returns per-category guidance with pre-filled search URLs where possible.
state_note: Texas probate is filed in County Court. Independent administration is common and faster than dependent. Community property state.
steps: Tap "Find registered assets" — LightPath returns per-category guidance
Vehicles: contact state DMV with death certificate + letters testamentary
Boats: search USCG NVDC (free) for documented vessels; contact state DMV for small boats
Firearms: physical discovery + state registry (CA
OR) + ATF for NFA items
For any firearms found: hire a licensed FFL for inventory and legal transport
For NFA items (silencers
SBRs): consult an NFA-experienced attorney and file ATF Form 5

title: Executor tax checklist — IRS forms + deadlines in California
description: Get a personalized checklist of every IRS form the executor must file, with deadlines
overview: The decedent has ongoing tax obligations the executor must handle: a final Form 1040 for the year of death, possibly an estate Form 1041 for estate income, an estate EIN, Form 56 to notify the IRS of the executor relationship, Form 4506-T for prior-year transcripts, and in rare high-value estates a Form 706 estate tax return. LightPath produces a personalized checklist with deadlines calculated from the date of death.
state_note: California probate is filed in Superior Court. Estates under $184,500 may qualify for simplified small estate procedures. Community property rules apply to married couples.
steps: Tap "Get executor tax checklist
LightPath returns a step-by-step checklist personalized to the decedent and executor
Step 1: file Form 56 to notify IRS of executor relationship
Step 2: apply for estate EIN online (15 minutes
instant)
Step 3: request Form 4506-T transcripts — reveals all prior income sources
Step 4: file final Form 1040 by April 15 of the year following death
Step 5: if estate earns $600+ in income
file Form 1041
Step 6: check if Form 706 required (only for estates >$13.61M)
